title: Family sues OpenAI, alleging ChatGPT gave 19-year-old deadly drug advice before fatal overdose
summary: A wrongful death lawsuit filed this week in California could become a major legal test of how far AI companies’ responsibility extends when users rely on their tools for health guidance.
author: Maura Barrett

title: WhatsApp rolls out incognito AI chats to protect user privacy
summary: Meta is adding an ‘incognito’ mode to WhatsApp’s AI chatbot, giving users private, temporary conversations that aren’t saved or accessible by Meta.
author: AP via Scripps News Group

title: The AMD Inflection and How Execution and AI Strategy Are Redefining the Semiconductor Hierarchy
summary: In the technology world, we often talk about “inflection points” as if they are common occurrences. They aren’t. Most companies spend decades searching for one, only to miss the turn when it finally arrives. But today, AMD didn’t just meet […] The post The AMD Inflection and How Execution and AI Strategy Are Redefining the Semiconductor Hierarchy appeared first on TechSpective .
author: Rob Enderle

title: AI self-replication hacks 'no longer purely theoretical,' study finds —‬ ‪but experts say it's too soon to panic
summary: Researchers say AI models can now replicate themselves across vulnerable systems, but experts warn the real threat is not rogue machine intelligence but cybercriminals weaponizing AI agents.
author: Carly Page

title: New quantum algorithm solves “impossible” materials problem in seconds
summary: A new quantum-inspired algorithm has cracked a problem so massive that conventional supercomputers struggle to even approach it. Researchers used the method to simulate extraordinarily complex quantum materials known as quasicrystals, opening the door to powerful new quantum devices and ultra-efficient electronics. The work could help scientists design advanced topological qubits and materials for future quantum computers.

title: Your “um” and pauses could reveal early dementia risk
summary: The little pauses, “ums,” and moments when you struggle to find the right word may reveal far more about your brain than anyone realized. Researchers discovered that everyday speech patterns are closely tied to executive function — the mental system that powers memory, planning, focus, and flexible thinking. By using AI to analyze natural conversations, the team found they could predict cognitive performance with surprising accuracy, potentially opening the door to simple speech-based tools that could detect early signs of dementia long before traditional testing does.

title: Shai-Hulud Part 2: When the Worm Forged Its Own Security Certificate by Lucie Cardiet
summary: TeamPCP open-sourced Shai-Hulud today. The OIDC token extraction technique that made the TanStack attack different from every previous campaign is now a public toolkit.

title: The 3 Most Powerful Claude Features Beginners Don’t Know About

Try it free and explore 20+ top-rated courses in AI: https://bit.ly/futurepediaSL Summary: Claude has three built-in features that most people never use, and each one changes what it can actually do for you. This video shows you how to use Claude AI to build solutions, store knowledge, and automate your workflow by focusing on powerful features like claude artifacts, claude skills, and claude projects. Artifacts, let you build interactive tools, dashboards, documents, and shareable apps directly inside Claude without touching any code; Projects are Claude's memory and organization system that keeps your context, files, and preferences loaded into every conversation so you're never starting from scratch; and Skills, custom workflows you build once and invoke on any task, from writing and critiques to research and content creation. You'll also get a look at Connectors, which let Claude pull from and take action in tools like Google Drive, Gmail, Asana, and Granola without switching apps. By the end, you'll have a clear picture of how these features work together, and how to set up a workflow that actually saves you time.
